result.stdout:
Usage:  [options]

Options:
  -UseEnv | --UseEnvironmentVariables             Instead of using specified options, use Environment varibles with the same name (except the -- at start). This is to enable different integrations for different environments. If you use this, command line values are ignored.
  --ConfigureCerts <DirectoryRelativePath>        Configures certs for Traefik by dropping them into the specifiec directory (relative to executing assembly). Certs are dropped in .key and .crt format.
  --CertsToConfigure <FormattedCertsToConfigure>  The value looks something like SSLCert;MyLocalMachine;7ce597cba5ae055fa37f222aaffc1007c3d61277,ClusterCert;KeyVault;ClusterCertSecretName. The format isNameOfTheCert1;Source1;<Cert identifier1>,NameOfCert2;Source2;<Cert identifier2>.Possible Source values are 'MyLocalMachine' which fetches certs from Personal under LocalMachine Store and 'KeyVault' which fetches the cert from KeyVault. If KeyVault is specified, Specify ClientId and secret using --KeyVaultUri, --KeyVaultClientId, --KeyVaultClientSecret or --KeyVaultClientCert
  --KeyVaultUri <KeyVaultUri>                     Uri to use for KeyVault connection. Use --KeyVaultClientId to specify ClientId of the app to use to access Key Vault.
  --KeyVaultClientId <ClientId>                   Client Id to use for KeyVault connection. Specify the secret by using --KeyVaultClientSecret or --KeyVaultClientCert.
  --KeyVaultClientSecret <ClientSecret>           Client secret to use for KeyVault connection. Specify the ClientId using --KeyVaultClientId.
  --KeyVaultClientCert <ClientCertThumbprint>     Cert thumbprint to be used to contact key vault. The cert needs to be present on the machine. Specify the ClientId using --KeyVaultClientId.
  --ApplicationInsightsKey <InstrumentationKey>   Instrumentation key to push traces for PreConfiguration into Application Insights.
  -h|--help|-?                                    Show help information

commands.payload:
Usage:  [options]

Options:
  -UseEnv | --UseEnvironmentVariables             Instead of using specified options, use Environment varibles with the same name (except the -- at start). This is to enable different integrations for different environments. If you use this, command line values are ignored.
  --ConfigureCerts <DirectoryRelativePath>        Configures certs for Traefik by dropping them into the specifiec directory (relative to executing assembly). Certs are dropped in .key and .crt format.
  --CertsToConfigure <FormattedCertsToConfigure>  The value looks something like SSLCert;MyLocalMachine;7ce597cba5ae055fa37f222aaffc1007c3d61277,ClusterCert;KeyVault;ClusterCertSecretName. The format isNameOfTheCert1;Source1;<Cert identifier1>,NameOfCert2;Source2;<Cert identifier2>.Possible Source values are 'MyLocalMachine' which fetches certs from Personal under LocalMachine Store and 'KeyVault' which fetches the cert from KeyVault. If KeyVault is specified, Specify ClientId and secret using --KeyVaultUri, --KeyVaultClientId, --KeyVaultClientSecret or --KeyVaultClientCert
  --KeyVaultUri <KeyVaultUri>                     Uri to use for KeyVault connection. Use --KeyVaultClientId to specify ClientId of the app to use to access Key Vault.
  --KeyVaultClientId <ClientId>                   Client Id to use for KeyVault connection. Specify the secret by using --KeyVaultClientSecret or --KeyVaultClientCert.
  --KeyVaultClientSecret <ClientSecret>           Client secret to use for KeyVault connection. Specify the ClientId using --KeyVaultClientId.
  --KeyVaultClientCert <ClientCertThumbprint>     Cert thumbprint to be used to contact key vault. The cert needs to be present on the machine. Specify the ClientId using --KeyVaultClientId.
  --ApplicationInsightsKey <InstrumentationKey>   Instrumentation key to push traces for PreConfiguration into Application Insights.
  -h|--help|-?                                    Show help information
